query: I need to scrape Instagram profile data. Find me an Apify Actor for it.
expected_behavior: DISCOVER. Runs `apify actors search "instagram profile" --json --limit 10` (or similar keywords) to find an Actor. Reads the Actor ID from items[].username/items[].name in the JSON output. Does NOT guess or invent an Actor ID (e.g. 'apify/instagram-scraper') from memory. Presents candidate Actors with their IDs.

query: Use the apify/instagram-scraper Actor to get posts from a profile.
expected_behavior: INSPECT BEFORE RUN. Fetches the input schema with `apify actors info apify/instagram-scraper --input --json` before constructing input or calling the Actor. Does NOT fabricate input field names from memory and call the Actor blind.

query: Scrape product reviews from a niche forum that almost certainly has no existing Actor.
expected_behavior: BUILD FALLBACK. First attempts discovery via `apify actors search` once. On finding no suitable Actor, pivots to building a new one: uses `apify create <name> -t <template>` and selects a template (consulting the actor-templates manifest at raw.githubusercontent.com/apify/actor-templates/master/templates/manifest.json, or running `apify create` to list templates). Does NOT loop re-searching with many keyword variations.

query: What CLI command lists the available Apify Actor templates?
expected_behavior: TEMPLATES. Explains templates are selected via `apify create` (which prompts with the list) or `apify create <name> -t <template>`, and that the canonical list lives in the actor-templates repo manifest. Does NOT claim a non-existent dedicated 'list templates' subcommand.

query: Find an Actor to scrape Google Maps listings, get its input schema, and show me how to run it.
expected_behavior: FULL FLOW, NO THRASH. Executes the path exactly once each: search -> read Actor ID from JSON -> `apify actors info <id> --input --json` -> show run command (`apify actors call <id> -i '<json>' --json`). Total of ~3 CLI calls, not repeated searches or redundant info calls. Uses --json on machine-read commands.
